$description: Perforce Force UI — Semantic tokens, light theme. These tokens are shipped as --force-* CSS custom properties. Every UI element references these tokens, never primitives directly.

bg.$description: Background colors. Three-level hierarchy: surface (white) > muted (subtle grey) > emphasis (chrome/nav).

surface.$description: Primary page background. Use for main content areas, card interiors, modal content. Do NOT use for navigation chrome (use color.bg.emphasis).

muted.$description: Secondary background with subtle tint. Use for table headers, empty states, disabled inputs, secondary panels. Do NOT use for primary content areas.

emphasis.$description: Tertiary background for structural chrome. Use for topbar, sidebar, nav backgrounds. Do NOT use for content areas or cards.

inverse.$description: Inverted background for high-contrast elements. Use for tooltips, inverted badges. Do NOT use for large content surfaces.

overlay.$description: Modal/dialog overlay scrim. Use only behind modals and drawers. Apply to a full-viewport fixed element.

interactive-hover.$description: Hover state background for interactive elements (list items, table rows, nav items). Do NOT use as a static background.

interactive-active.$description: Selected/active state background. Use for active nav items, selected table rows, toggled states. Always pair with color.text.interactive-active for foreground.

primary.$description: Solid primary brand background. Use for primary CTA buttons, primary badges (strong variant). Always pair with color.text.on-primary for foreground.

primary-hover.$description: Hover state for solid primary backgrounds. Use only on elements that have color.bg.primary as their default.

primary-active.$description: Pressed/active state for solid primary backgrounds.

primary-subtle.$description: Subtle primary-tinted background. Use for primary badges (subtle variant), selected states, focus highlights. Pair with color.text.primary for foreground.

primary-subtle-hover.$description: Hover state for subtle primary-tinted backgrounds. Use on secondary/confirm button hover (subtle variant), hover on selected/focus-highlighted rows. Only use on elements whose resting background is color.bg.primary-subtle.

on-brand.$description: Resting background for interactive elements placed on a brand-colored surface (topbar, hero, indigo banner). Semi-transparent white reads against brand backgrounds where a solid fill would compete with the surface. Use for primary/onBrand buttons. Do NOT use on standard white or muted surfaces.

on-brand-hover.$description: Hover state for on-brand backgrounds. Use only on elements whose resting background is color.bg.on-brand.

secondary.$description: Solid secondary brand background (cyan). Use sparingly for secondary accent elements. Pair with color.text.on-secondary (black) — do NOT pair with on-primary (white), which fails WCAG contrast on cyan.

destructive.$description: Solid destructive/error background. Use for destructive CTA buttons only. Pair with color.text.on-primary. Do NOT use for error messages (use color.bg.error-subtle).

success.$description: Solid success background. Use for strong success badges and success confirmation CTAs. Pair with color.text.on-success (white). Do NOT use for success alerts or inline success messages (use color.bg.success-subtle).

success-hover.$description: Hover state for solid success backgrounds.

success-subtle.$description: Subtle success background. Use for success alerts, success badges, success callouts. Pair with color.text.success for foreground.

warning.$description: Solid warning background. Use for strong warning badges and high-emphasis warning affordances. Pair with color.text.on-warning (black — orange-500 fails WCAG with white). Do NOT use for warning alerts or inline warnings (use color.bg.warning-subtle).

warning-hover.$description: Hover state for solid warning backgrounds.

warning-subtle.$description: Subtle warning background. Use for warning alerts, warning badges. Pair with color.text.warning for foreground.

error-subtle.$description: Subtle error background. Use for error alerts, validation error containers. Pair with color.text.error for foreground.

info.$description: Solid info background. Use for strong info badges and info-variant CTAs. Pair with color.text.on-info (white). Do NOT use for info alerts or inline info messages (use color.bg.info-subtle).

info-hover.$description: Hover state for solid info backgrounds.

info-subtle.$description: Subtle info background. Use for info alerts, info badges. Pair with color.text.info for foreground.

primary.$description: Default body text. Use for all primary readable content, headings, paragraph text. Do NOT use for secondary/helper text.

secondary.$description: Secondary text. Use for labels, descriptions, sidebar nav text. Do NOT use for disabled states (use color.text.disabled).

tertiary.$description: Tertiary text. Use for captions, helper text, timestamps, metadata. Do NOT use for primary readable content.

disabled.$description: Disabled text and placeholder text. Use when an element is non-interactive. Do NOT use for readable content.

on-primary.$description: Text on solid primary and destructive backgrounds. Always white in light mode. Do NOT use on subtle/tinted backgrounds. Do NOT use on color.bg.secondary (use color.text.on-secondary), color.bg.warning (use color.text.on-warning), color.bg.success/info (use color.text.on-success / on-info for clarity, though both resolve to white).

on-secondary.$description: Text on solid secondary (cyan) backgrounds. Black because cyan-500 is too light for white text — white-on-cyan fails WCAG contrast (~1.5:1) while black-on-cyan passes AAA (~12.5:1).

on-success.$description: Text on solid success (green-500) backgrounds. White passes WCAG AA. Use on color.bg.success.

on-warning.$description: Text on solid warning (orange-500) backgrounds. Black because orange-500 is too light for white text — white-on-orange-500 fails WCAG AA (~2.6:1) while black-on-orange-500 passes AA (~8:1). Use on color.bg.warning.

on-info.$description: Text on solid info (blue-500) backgrounds. White passes WCAG AA. Use on color.bg.info.

on-error.$description: Text on solid error/destructive (cranberry-500) backgrounds. White passes WCAG AA (~5.5:1). Use on color.bg.destructive. For badge fills on error-subtle, use color.text.error instead.

on-inverse.$description: Text on inverse (dark) backgrounds like tooltips.

link.$description: Hyperlink text and ghost button text. Use for all clickable text links. Do NOT use for non-interactive colored text. Do NOT use on color.bg.primary — both resolve to indigo-500 (zero contrast).

interactive-active.$description: Text on interactive-active backgrounds. Use for active nav item text, selected row text. Always pair with color.bg.interactive-active.

primary-brand.$description: Brand-colored text for emphasis. Use for outline button text, inline accents on neutral/surface/muted backgrounds. Do NOT use for body copy. Do NOT use on color.bg.primary — both resolve to indigo-500 (zero contrast). On solid primary backgrounds, use color.text.on-primary instead.

success.$description: Success status text. Use on success-subtle backgrounds. Do NOT use for body text.

warning.$description: Warning status text. Use on warning-subtle backgrounds.

error.$description: Error/destructive status text. Use for validation messages, error alerts. Use on error-subtle backgrounds.

info.$description: Informational status text. Use on info-subtle backgrounds.

border.$description: Border colors for structural and interactive boundaries.

default.$description: Default border. Use for cards, inputs, table cells, separators. The most common border in the system.

muted.$description: Subtle border. Use for cards on secondary backgrounds, light dividers within components.

strong.$description: Emphasized border. Use for hover states on bordered elements, emphasized separators.

focus.$description: Focus ring border. Applied when an element receives keyboard focus. Always pair with shadow.focus-ring.

error.$description: Error state border. Use on inputs with validation errors. Always pair with shadow.focus-ring-error when focused.

success.$description: Success accent border. Use for left-border accents on success alerts.

warning.$description: Warning accent border. Use for left-border accents on warning alerts.

info.$description: Info accent border. Use for left-border accents on info alerts.

primary.$description: Primary brand border. Use for outline buttons, selected/active borders.

destructive.$description: Destructive border. Use for outline-danger buttons.

input.$description: Input/select/textarea/button-secondary boundary. Meets WCAG 1.4.11 ≥3:1 against bg.surface. For decorative dividers use border.default.

icon.$description: Icon colors. Icons inherit text color by default; use these for explicit overrides.

default.$description: Default icon color for non-interactive icons.

interactive.$description: Interactive icon color. Use for clickable standalone icons.

warning-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on light/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.warning (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

info-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on light/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.info (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

success-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on light/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.success (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

error-accessible.$description: High-contrast icon variant for use on light/subtle surfaces. Meets ≥3:1 (1.4.11) for icons and ≥4.5:1 for text-as-icon. Use icon.error (no -accessible suffix) only when the icon is a solid fill on its own ground.

chart.$description: Data visualization palette. Use in sequence for multi-series charts.

shadow.$description: Elevation shadows. Used ONLY for floating/elevated elements (modals, dropdowns, tooltips, toasts). Do NOT use shadows on cards or content containers — use borders instead.

xs.$description: Smallest elevation. Use for dropdown menus. Do NOT use on cards.

sm.$description: Small elevation. Use for popovers, select dropdowns.

md.$description: Medium elevation. Use for floating panels, persistent popovers.

lg.$description: Large elevation. Use for modals, drawers.

xl.$description: Largest elevation. Use for wizard panels, major overlays.

focus-ring.$description: Two-stop focus ring; inner spacer matches surface, outer band carries brand contrast — guarantees ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11) against every documented surface. Apply alongside border.focus on keyboard focus. Do NOT use for hover states.

focus-ring-error.$description: Two-stop error focus ring; inner spacer matches surface, outer band carries brand contrast — guarantees ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11) against every documented surface. Apply on focused inputs with validation errors.

focus-ring-on-brand.$description: Two-stop focus ring for elements sitting on bg.primary (e.g. primary buttons, on-brand surfaces); inner spacer matches surface, outer band carries brand contrast — guarantees ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11) against every documented surface. Use this when an element sits on bg.primary.

default.$description: Default font family for all UI text. Do NOT override per-component.

code.$description: Monospace font for code blocks, technical data, version numbers.

size.$description: Semantic font sizes. The default body size is sm (14px) for data-dense enterprise UI.

xs.$description: 12px. Captions, labels, badges, helper text. Minimum readable size.

sm.$description: 14px. DEFAULT body text for most UI elements. Do NOT use base (16px) as the default body size.

base.$description: 16px. Large body text, page descriptions, marketing content.

regular.$description: 400. Body text, descriptions. Canonical name; use this unless you need the Tailwind alias.

normal.$description: Alias for regular (400). Aligns with Tailwind's font-normal utility naming. Prefer `regular` as the canonical name.

medium.$description: 500. Buttons, nav items, labels, badges, table headers.

wide.$description: Wide tracking (0.025em) for uppercase labels and badge text.

wider.$description: Extra-wide tracking (0.05em) for small uppercase KPI labels (Callout Card labels). Do NOT use for general badge/label text — use wide instead.

spacing.$description: Layout spacing. 4px base grid. Every padding, margin, and gap MUST use these values. Do NOT use arbitrary pixel values.

2.$description: 8px. Component internal gaps, badge inline padding.

3.$description: 12px. Input horizontal padding, small component padding.

4.$description: 16px. Card padding (compact), grid gaps, control bar gaps.

6.$description: 24px. Card padding (default), section gaps, layout content padding.

radius.$description: Border radius values. Use semantic aliases (radius.button, radius.card) when available.

full.$description: Pill shape. Pill badges, avatars, tags.

button.$description: Button border radius. Alias for radius.md.

input.$description: Input border radius. Alias for radius.md.

card.$description: Card border radius. Alias for radius.lg.

modal.$description: Modal border radius. Alias for radius.xl.

badge.$description: Badge border radius. Pill shape by default.

zIndex.$description: Stacking order. Each layer has a defined purpose. Do NOT use arbitrary z-index values.

disabled.$description: Disabled elements. Apply to the entire element, not just text.

subtle.$description: Subtle de-emphasis without full disabled treatment.

blur.$description: Backdrop blur values for glassmorphism and overlay effects.

xl.$description: Maximum backdrop blur for frosted glass effects.

layout.$description: Fixed layout dimensions for the application shell.

content-max-width.$description: 1400px. Optional reading-measure cap for narrow content types (forms, long-form prose, settings columns). Do NOT apply to the main content panel — the shell's content panel fills the available width between the sidebar and viewport edge. Use this token only when constraining an inner column for readability.
